evidence: R1 running-config: routing ip-route-interface-forwarding-list entry: prefix "172.16.0.0" mask "255.255.255.0" fwd-list fwd "10.0.0.9" (call lc_941f6e1e…)
R3 running-config: routing ip-route-interface-forwarding-list entry: prefix "172.16.0.0" mask "255.255.255.0" fwd-list fwd "10.0.0.10" (call lc_f77b1ecc…)

OSPF / interface state shows R1<->R3 link is up (R1 OSPF neighbor on Serial0/1/1 neighbor_address 10.0.0.9; R3 OSPF shows neighbor 1.1.1.1@10.0.0.10) — so packets will be forwarded between them (call lc_90e6..., lc_d668d4...)

No interface on R1 or R3 shows an IP in 172.16.0.0/24 in their running-configs (R1 interfaces: 192.168.0.1, 10.0.0.1, 10.0.0.10; R3 interfaces: 203.0.113.1, 10.0.0.9, 10.0.0.6) (call lc_941f6e1e…, lc_f77b1ecc…)

Both routers instead have explicit static/route-forwarding entries for 172.16.0.0/24 that refer to each other (R1 -> 10.0.0.9; R3 -> 10.0.0.10), indicating the prefix is not directly connected to either device (call lc_941f6e1e…, lc_f77b1ecc…)

plan_history.intent_description: Investigate ping timeouts: source 192.168.0.12 cannot reach destination 172.16.0.12 (25 pings sent, 0 successful). Goal: identify root cause of reachability failure and restore connectivity.
plan_history.problem: Mutual/static next-hop routing loop for 172.16.0.0/24: R1 has static route to 172.16.0.0/24 via 10.0.0.9 and R3 has static route to 172.16.0.0/24 via 10.0.0.10, causing traffic to be forwarded between them and never reach the destination. Missing info: actual attachment point or authoritative next-hop for 172.16.0.0/24 (identify which device/net hosts 172.16.0.0/24 or the correct next-hop for 172.16.0.12).
plan_history.fix_summary: Remove the incorrect reciprocal static routes that create the forwarding loop so routing can converge correctly (and then, once the authoritative next-hop/attachment for 172.16.0.0/24 is identified, add a correct route if needed).

remaining_issues: The prefix 172.16.0.0/24 is not advertised or attached on any collected device — no authoritative next-hop or interface for 172.16.0.0/24 was found.
Because the prefix is not present, hosts in 172.16.0.0/24 (e.g., 172.16.0.12) will still be unreachable until an authoritative attachment or correct route is added.
